hazardous_situation: device reports normal result when patient condition is worsening
suggested_control: sensor calibration, alarm validation, clinical verification

hazardous_situation: prolonged sensor contact causes localized skin irritation or allergic response
harm: contact dermatitis, chemical burn, or pressure injury
possible_causes: biocompatible material failure
prolonged wear beyond recommended duration
individual skin sensitivity not assessed
risk_controls: use ISO 10993-compliant biocompatible adhesive
label maximum wear duration clearly
include contraindication for known adhesive allergies in IFU

hazardous_situation: sensor data fails to reach monitoring system during a critical clinical event
harm: undetected patient deterioration, delayed clinical intervention
possible_causes: Bluetooth or Wi-Fi interference in clinical environment
battery depletion without user awareness
gateway connectivity loss
risk_controls: local data buffering with automatic retry logic
low-battery audible and visual alarm
connection-loss alert to clinical staff
verification_methods: battery life validation across environmental conditions

hazardous_situation: pump delivers medication at incorrect flow rate due to programming error or mechanical fault
harm: drug overdose or underdose leading to adverse drug reaction or treatment failure
possible_causes: clinician programming error on touchscreen
occlusion upstream causing dose stacking on clearance
worn pump mechanism delivering inaccurate volume
risk_controls: dose error reduction software with configurable hard limits
occlusion detection alarm with automatic pause
independent double-check workflow for high-alert medications

hazardous_situation: air bubble enters IV line without triggering the air-in-line detector
harm: air embolism, cardiac arrest
possible_causes: empty infusion bag not detected in time
tubing not properly primed before use
detector sensitivity degraded after repeated use
risk_controls: ultrasonic air-in-line detector with audible and visual alarm
empty-bag detection with automatic stop
priming procedure required before pump activation
verification_methods: air detection sensitivity testing per IEC 60601-2-24

hazardous_situation: infusion set allows uncontrolled gravity flow when disconnected from pump
harm: rapid drug overdose
possible_causes: anti-free-flow clamp not engaged by user
single-use set reused after clamp spring fatigue
damaged clamp mechanism not detected before use
risk_controls: anti-free-flow design requiring active clamp engagement
incompatibility with gravity administration by design
mandatory pre-use clamp inspection step in IFU

hazardous_situation: device reports blood glucose within normal range when patient is hypoglycemic
harm: failure to treat hypoglycemia resulting in loss of consciousness or seizure
possible_causes: hematocrit interference outside validated range
expired test strip used
insufficient blood sample volume applied
risk_controls: hematocrit correction algorithm with flagging outside range
strip expiry date detection with blocking error code
insufficient sample volume detection before result display

hazardous_situation: device reading drifts over time without user awareness
harm: systematic dosing errors leading to chronic hyperglycemia or hypoglycemia
possible_causes: electrochemical sensor aging beyond validated period
temperature or humidity outside specified storage conditions
user skips quality control checks
risk_controls: mandatory calibration or warm-up reminder at startup
built-in quality control check strips with expected value range
sensor or strip expiry alarm

hazardous_situation: patient applies insufficient or contaminated blood sample to test strip
harm: inaccurate reading leads to inappropriate insulin dose
possible_causes: inadequate user training for self-monitoring
complex interface for elderly or visually impaired users
instructions for use not available in user's language
risk_controls: audible sample sufficiency confirmation before result display
simplified step-by-step graphical user interface
multilingual instructions for use
verification_methods: human factors study with intended user population

hazardous_situation: cuff applied over clothing or at incorrect position on arm
harm: inaccurate BP reading resulting in missed hypertension or false positive diagnosis
possible_causes: positioning instructions unclear for home user
wide range of arm circumferences not addressed by single cuff
user self-measuring without any training
risk_controls: cuff position indicator with user feedback
cuff size selection guide in labeling
on-device animated positioning instruction
verification_methods: usability study with intended lay user population

hazard: irregular heartbeat interference with oscillometric algorithm
hazardous_situation: arrhythmia during measurement causes algorithm to output erroneous BP value
harm: falsely elevated or depressed BP reading leading to inappropriate treatment decision
possible_causes: oscillometric algorithm not validated in arrhythmia population
atrial fibrillation highly prevalent in hypertensive target population
risk_controls: irregular heartbeat indicator flag displayed with measurement
IFU recommendation to confirm reading with clinician when flag is shown
clinical algorithm validation including arrhythmia subjects
verification_methods: clinical validation study including AF population

hazardous_situation: device powers off mid-measurement for patient with uncontrolled hypertension
harm: missed reading delays detection and treatment of hypertensive crisis
possible_causes: no low-battery warning before depletion
battery drains faster in cold home environment
user reliant on single device without backup
risk_controls: low-battery visual and audible warning at sufficient remaining capacity
battery performance tested across operating temperature range
IFU guidance on battery maintenance frequency

hazardous_situation: dry or displaced electrode produces high-impedance artifact masking ECG waveform
harm: missed arrhythmia or ST-elevation leading to delayed cardiac intervention
possible_causes: electrode drying during extended monitoring
patient movement dislodging electrode
inadequate skin preparation before electrode placement
risk_controls: lead-off detection alarm with audible and visual alert
impedance monitoring with alert threshold
skin preparation instructions in IFU
verification_methods: signal quality validation across impedance range

hazardous_situation: automated arrhythmia detection outputs false negative for ventricular fibrillation
harm: alarm not triggered, fatal arrhythmia untreated
possible_causes: algorithm training data not representative of all waveform morphologies
noise rejection threshold set too aggressively
algorithm not validated for pediatric patient population
risk_controls: algorithm validated on diverse annotated ECG database
secondary confirmation mechanism before suppressing critical alarm
clinician manual override capability

hazardous_situation: ECG lead detaches during unattended monitoring without triggering alarm
harm: cardiac event undetected, delayed resuscitation
possible_causes: cable fatigue at strain relief point
inadequate lead retention mechanism for active patients
alarm silenced by staff and not re-enabled
risk_controls: lead-off alarm with automatic escalation after silence timeout
reinforced lead connectors with positive-latch design
alarm management policy documentation

hazardous_situation: cutting or grasping mechanism fails mid-operation
harm: unintended tissue damage, hemorrhage, prolonged surgical procedure
possible_causes: material fatigue from exceeding validated reprocessing cycle count
assembly defect introduced during manufacturing
use of instrument beyond its validated application
risk_controls: cycle count marking on instrument body
materials and mechanical testing across lifecycle
pre-use inspection checklist in IFU

hazardous_situation: instrument reaches patient with residual biological contamination after reprocessing
harm: surgical site infection, sepsis
possible_causes: complex internal geometry trapping debris after use
reprocessing instructions not validated for all cleaning equipment types
user deviates from validated cleaning protocol
risk_controls: validated cleaning and sterilization instructions per ISO 17664
design-for-cleanability review with simulated soil testing
reprocessing training program for sterile processing staff

hazardous_situation: a component breaks off and is retained in the surgical site
harm: retained foreign body requiring secondary intervention, infection
possible_causes: stress concentration at thin cross-section under load
brittle fracture from sub-critical material defect
inadequate pre-use visual inspection
risk_controls: design robustness analysis (DFMEA) at fracture-risk features
surgical count and inspection protocol for all components
radiopaque markers for X-ray detectability of fragments

hazardous_situation: implanted device reaches end-of-life and stops therapy delivery
harm: sudden loss of pacing or neurostimulation therapy, life-threatening event
possible_causes: battery longevity shorter than predicted due to high therapy demand
patient missed scheduled follow-up appointment
remote monitoring not configured at implant
risk_controls: elective replacement indicator with early warning threshold communicated to clinician
remote monitoring telemetry with automated alert
patient education on follow-up intervals
verification_methods: battery longevity testing under maximum demand conditions

hazardous_situation: lead breaks and interrupts electrical continuity between device and target tissue
harm: loss of pacing or sensing, arrhythmia, inappropriate shock delivery
possible_causes: repetitive mechanical stress at subclavian crush point
manufacturing defect in conductor coil
patient anatomy creating abnormal lead stress
risk_controls: lead integrity monitoring algorithm with patient alert
material and mechanical durability testing across fatigue cycles
implant technique guidelines minimizing stress concentration
verification_methods: mechanical fatigue testing per applicable standard

hazardous_situation: patient undergoes MRI without following MRI conditional labeling requirements
harm: device malfunction, cardiac tissue heating, device movement or dislodgement
possible_causes: patient not aware of implant MRI restrictions
radiology staff not informed of implant before scan
MRI parameters used outside MRI-conditional labeling
risk_controls: patient implant card listing MRI conditions
MRI-conditional programming mode activated before scan
pre-scan MRI screening protocol for clinical staff
verification_methods: MRI conditional testing per ASTM F2182 and ISO/TS 10974

hazardous_situation: AI diagnostic model performs significantly worse on patient subgroups underrepresented in training data
harm: missed diagnosis or false positive in specific demographic groups
possible_causes: non-diverse training dataset lacking minority demographics
no subgroup performance analysis during development
image quality or skin tone variation not accounted for in model
risk_controls: subgroup performance analysis required during development and validation
diverse and representative training and validation dataset
mandatory clinician review of all AI diagnostic outputs

hazardous_situation: software update introduces error in analysis algorithm undetected before deployment
harm: systematically incorrect diagnostic outputs affecting large number of patients
possible_causes: inadequate automated regression testing coverage
no version control on model artifacts
update deployed without clinical re-validation
risk_controls: automated regression test suite against reference dataset before each release
change control procedure with risk assessment
staged rollout with post-deployment performance monitoring
verification_methods: regression testing on validated reference dataset

hazardous_situation: software receives incorrect patient data from EHR due to interface mapping error
harm: diagnosis based on wrong patient data, potential patient misidentification
possible_causes: HL7 or FHIR field mapping error between systems
field name mismatch between EHR and software
unit conversion error in data normalization
risk_controls: input data validation and clinical range sanity checks
patient identifier cross-verification before analysis
data provenance logging for audit trail
verification_methods: interface integration testing with real EHR sandbox

hazardous_situation: app sends medication reminder at wrong time due to timezone or daylight saving time error
harm: missed dose or double dose leading to therapeutic failure or drug toxicity
possible_causes: timezone handling bug in scheduling logic
daylight saving time transition not handled
server and device timezone mismatch
risk_controls: timezone-aware scheduling with UTC normalization
dose logging with timestamp to detect missed or duplicate doses
user-accessible reminder review and edit interface
verification_methods: timezone and DST boundary testing across regions

hazard: app crash during symptom data entry
hazardous_situation: critical symptom data is lost before transmission to clinical team
harm: delayed clinical response to patient deterioration
possible_causes: memory leak on target mobile OS version
database write error during sync
network timeout causing uncaught exception
risk_controls: local auto-save before any network transmission
crash reporting and session recovery mechanism
data sync retry logic with user notification
verification_methods: stress and crash testing on target devices
data integrity testing under network interruption

hazard: unauthorized access to patient health data
hazardous_situation: weak authentication allows unauthorized user to access patient health records stored in app
harm: patient privacy breach, potential misuse of sensitive health data
possible_causes: no biometric or two-factor authentication enforced
session token not invalidated on logout
insecure local data storage without encryption
risk_controls: mandatory strong authentication with biometric or 2FA option
encrypted local storage for all health data
session timeout and token invalidation on logout

hazardous_situation: kinking or thrombus formation blocks fluid flow through catheter lumen
harm: failure of drug delivery or drainage, treatment delay
possible_causes: catheter material kinking at anatomical bend points
blood backflow due to inadequate flushing protocol
tip malposition increasing occlusion risk
risk_controls: kink-resistant catheter material selection
anti-thrombogenic surface coating
flushing protocol and frequency specified in IFU

hazardous_situation: catheter tip moves from intended anatomical position after initial placement
harm: fluid delivered to unintended anatomical site, vessel damage, pneumothorax
possible_causes: inadequate external fixation after placement
patient movement dislodging catheter
tip design not optimized for retention in target vessel
risk_controls: securement device requirement stated in IFU
radiopaque tip marker for position verification by imaging
tip position confirmation protocol before use
verification_methods: tip migration testing in simulated anatomy model

hazardous_situation: skin microorganisms migrate along catheter tract after insertion
harm: catheter-related bloodstream infection, sepsis
possible_causes: break in aseptic technique during insertion
prolonged indwelling duration beyond recommended maximum
hub contamination during access
risk_controls: antimicrobial catheter surface coating
sterile insertion kit with procedural checklist
maximum recommended dwell time stated in IFU
verification_methods: clinical infection rate monitoring in post-market surveillance

hazardous_situation: breathing circuit detaches from patient airway without triggering an alarm
harm: complete loss of ventilation, hypoxia, cardiac arrest
possible_causes: low-pressure alarm threshold set too low or disabled by staff
connector design allows unintentional pull-out under low force
alarm silenced and timeout not enforced
risk_controls: low-pressure disconnect alarm with non-defeatable minimum threshold
positive-locking circuit connectors
alarm re-enable after fixed silence timeout

hazardous_situation: ventilator delivers tidal volume significantly outside prescribed range due to sensor drift or compliance mismatch
harm: barotrauma from over-inflation or hypoventilation from under-delivery
possible_causes: patient circuit compliance not compensated in volume calculation
flow sensor drift over time
clinician sets incorrect weight-based tidal volume
risk_controls: circuit compliance compensation algorithm with auto-measurement
flow sensor auto-calibration at startup
weight-based tidal volume calculator with out-of-range alert
verification_methods: delivered volume accuracy testing across lung compliance range

hazardous_situation: FiO2 delivered to patient deviates significantly from set value
harm: hyperoxia causing lung injury or retinopathy, or hypoxia
possible_causes: oxygen sensor failure with no redundant sensing
blender mechanism fault under supply pressure variation
gas supply pressure drop below minimum operating pressure
risk_controls: redundant oxygen sensing with alarm on deviation from set value
blender performance tested across validated supply pressure range
low supply pressure alarm

hazardous_situation: patient misunderstands device output and takes inappropriate self-treatment action
harm: delayed emergency care or inappropriate medication adjustment
possible_causes: clinical terminology displayed not accessible to lay user
no guidance on when to seek emergency care
ambiguous trend display without context
risk_controls: plain-language display with actionable guidance for each result state
threshold-based alert with explicit seek emergency care instruction
user interface designed and tested with intended lay population
verification_methods: summative usability study with intended lay users

hazardous_situation: device malfunctions and continues displaying readings without alerting user to fault state
harm: patient relies on incorrect readings, undetected clinical deterioration
possible_causes: no built-in self-diagnostic test
fault masked by stale displayed value from last successful measurement
no connectivity-based remote device health monitoring
risk_controls: periodic self-diagnostic with fault indicator on display
stale data timeout with warning displayed
remote alert to care team on detected device fault

hazardous_situation: device fails to transmit patient data to clinical team due to home connectivity loss
harm: clinician makes decisions based on outdated or missing patient data
possible_causes: Wi-Fi dropout in home environment
app installed on unsupported operating system version
cellular data plan expired on device
risk_controls: local data buffering with automatic retry on reconnection
transmission failure alert to both patient and care team
minimum connectivity requirement specified in IFU
